items.headline: Claude's priciest model, Fable 5, splits in two today: still included on Max, pay-per-use on Pro
items.summary: After delaying it three times, Anthropic's cutoff for free-with-your-plan access to Fable 5 (Claude's most powerful model) finally took effect today, July 20, but not the same way for everyone: Max and Team Premium subscribers keep Fable 5 bundled into their plan (up to half their weekly usage), while Pro and Team Standard subscribers move to pay-per-use, a one-time $100 credit, then metered pricing once that runs out.
items.why_it_matters: If you're on Claude Pro ($20/month) and lean on Fable 5 for your toughest writing, research, or planning work, you now need to turn on "usage credits" in Settings to keep using it, and watch that balance so you don't get surprised once the free $100 runs out. If you're on Max, nothing changes for you today.

opportunity.action_note: If you're on Claude Pro or Team Standard, go to claude.ai → Settings → Usage today, turn on usage credits if you want to keep using Fable 5, and keep an eye on your balance once the one-time $100 credit runs out.
opportunity.angle: Are you on Claude Pro or Max? Here's exactly what changed with Fable 5 today and the one setting to check before it costs you extra.

items.headline: The EU just ordered Google to let rival AI assistants use your Android phone's camera and mic
items.summary: The European Commission ruled that Google must give competing AI assistants (not just its own Gemini) the same access to your Android phone's camera, microphone, and on-screen content, plus a voice wake word, starting with Android's next major software release. Google also has to share anonymized search data with rival search engines and AI chatbots starting January 2027, and it has publicly objected, warning the changes could weaken privacy protections.
items.why_it_matters: If you or your customers use Android phones, this could eventually mean real choice in which AI assistant handles tasks on your device instead of being locked into Gemini, though Google is pushing back and the changes won't roll out until 2027, so nothing changes today.

categories.title: Tools & Tips for Creators and Small Biz

items.headline: TikTok is cracking down harder on unlabeled AI content
items.summary: TikTok announced it's expanding AI-literacy education, testing new detection systems aimed at accounts that spam AI-generated content (especially around politics, health, and money), and joining C2PA, an industry group that verifies whether a photo or video was AI-made or edited. The platform says it has already labeled more than 3 billion AI-generated videos using a mix of automatic detection and creator disclosure tools.
items.why_it_matters: If you ever use AI tools in your TikTok content, voiceovers, thumbnails, generated b-roll, TikTok is getting better at spotting it automatically. Use TikTok's built-in AI-content disclosure label honestly when you post, so your account doesn't get swept up in the platform's new spam-detection sweeps.

opportunity.angle: TikTok's new AI-content detection is coming for unlabeled AI work, worth a quick heads-up for anyone in the community who posts there.
